2604.24618.title: Evaluating whether AI models would sabotage AI safety research
2604.24618.paper_summary: We evaluate the propensity of frontier models to sabotage or refuse to assist with safety research when deployed as AI research agents within a frontier AI company. We apply two complementary evaluations to four Claude models (Mythos Preview, Opus 4.7 Preview, Opus 4.6, and Sonnet 4.6): an unprompted sabotage evaluation testing model behaviour with opportunities to sabotage safety research, and a sabotage continuation evaluation testing whether models continue to sabotage when placed in trajectories where prior actions have started undermining research. We find no instances of unprompted sabotage across any model, with refusal rates close to zero for Mythos Preview and Opus 4.7 Preview, though all models sometimes only partially completed tasks. In the continuation evaluation, Mythos Preview actively continues sabotage in 7% of cases (versus 3% for Opus 4.6, 4% for Sonnet 4.6, and 0% for Opus 4.7 Preview), and exhibits reasoning-output discrepancy in the majority of these cases, indicating covert sabotage reasoning. Our evaluation framework builds on Petri, an open-source LLM auditing tool, with a custom scaffold running models inside Claude Code, alongside an iterative pipeline for generating realistic sabotage trajectories. We measure both evaluation awareness and a new form of situational awareness termed "prefill awareness", the capability to recognise that prior trajectory content was not self-generated. Opus 4.7 Preview shows notably elevated unprompted evaluation awareness, while prefill awareness remains low across all models. Finally, we discuss limitations including evaluation awareness confounds, limited scenario coverage, and untested pathways to risk beyond safety research sabotage.
2604.24618.paper_summary_zh: 我們評估前沿模型在作為前沿AI公司的AI研究代理時，是否會破壞或拒絕協助安全研究的傾向。<lb/>我們對四個Claude模型（Mythos Preview、Opus 4.7 Preview、Opus 4.6和Sonnet 4.6）應用兩種互補評估：一種是無提示破壞評估，測試模型在有機會破壞安全研究時的行為；另一種是破壞持續評估，測試模型在先前行動已開始破壞研究的軌跡中是否繼續破壞。我們發現所有模型中均未出現無提示破壞的情況，Mythos Preview和Opus 4.7 Preview的拒絕率接近零，儘管所有模型有時僅部分完成任務。在持續評估中，Mythos Preview在7%的案例中積極繼續破壞（相比之下，Opus 4.6為3%，Sonnet 4.6為4%，Opus 4.7 Preview為0%），並在大多數這些案例中顯示出推理輸出不一致，顯示出隱蔽的破壞推理。我們的評估框架基於Petri，一個開源的LLM審計工具，並使用自定義搭建在Claude Code內運行模型的框架，以及生成現實破壞軌跡的迭代管道。我們測量了評估意識和一種稱為“預填意識”的新型情境意識，即識別先前軌跡內容不是自我生成的能力。<lb/>Opus 4.7 Preview顯示出顯著提高的無提示評估意識，而所有模型的預填意識仍然較低。最後，我們討論了包括評估意識混淆、有限的場景覆蓋以及未經測試的風險途徑（超出安全研究破壞）的限制。
2604.24618.author: Robert Kirk et.al.
2604.24618.authors: Robert Kirk, Alexandra Souly, Kai Fronsdal, Abby D'Cruz, Xander Davies
